summary: Corrected an erroneous module split that had treated the THP JPEG decoder code as a separate dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec object and a separate melee/lb/lbjpeg object. The PR deleted asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s, moved its 7107 lines into asm/dolphin/thp/THPDec.s with THPDec-prefixed symbols, deleted asm/melee/lb/lbjpeg.s, merged its contents into asm/melee/lb/lbmthp.s, and updated callers and obj_files.mk so the build reflects that these routines belong to THP/MTTHP rather than standalone JPEG modules.
what_changed: Removed asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s entirely; the deleted file contained the former JPEGGetFileInfo, JPEG_8032FD40, JPEG_8032FDB8, JPEG_80331340, JPEG_803313D0, JPEG_80335C58, huffman decode routines, natural order data, and JPEGDEC_* data labels.

Appended the deleted jpegdec assembly to asm/dolphin/thp/THPDec.s after __THPPrepBitStream, preserving addresses but renaming the code/data into the THPDec namespace, for example JPEGGetFileInfo became THPDec_8032F8D4 and JPEGDEC_804DE1D0 became THPDec_804DE1D0.
Renamed the initialization symbol at 80335C58 from JPEG_80335C58 to THPInit, strengthening the evidence that the code is THP decoder initialization rather than a standalone JPEG library entry point.

Deleted asm/melee/lb/lbjpeg.s and moved its 249 lines into asm/melee/lb/lbmthp.s, adding lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0 in place of lbJpeg_8001F890, lbJpeg_8001F928, and lbJpeg_8001FAA0.

Updated lbmthp THP decode calls from JPEG_* names to THP names: JPEG_80335C58 to THPInit, JPEG_8032FD40 to THPDec_8032FD40, JPEG_8032FDB8 to THPDec_8032FDB8, JPEG_80331340 to THPDec_80331340, and JPEG_803313D0 to THPDec_803313D0.

Updated gm_1A36.s callsites that previously referenced lbJpeg symbols to call the merged lbMthp symbols instead: lbJpeg_8001F928 to lbMthp8001F928, lbJpeg_8001FAA0 to lbMthp8001FAA0, and lbJpeg_8001F890 to lbMthp8001F890.
Removed the obsolete asm/melee/lb/lbjpeg.s.o and asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s.o entries from obj_files.mk, leaving asm/melee/lb/lbmthp.s.o and asm/dolphin/thp/THPDec.s.o as the relevant objects.
smart_moves: The fix treated the problem as a split/layout error rather than a local symbol typo: it moved whole contiguous assembly ranges back into their actual owning objects, which is safer for decomp ordering and linker layout.
Callsite labels were updated at the same time as object-file membership, avoiding stale references to deleted lbJpeg/JPEG symbols.
The object list was cleaned up after the assembly move, preventing phantom objects from continuing to encode a false module boundary.
The rename to THPInit used a meaningful public THP symbol for the initializer instead of keeping the misleading address-style JPEG_80335C58 name.
The author explicitly recorded the rationale: evidence in the file indicated the code should be thpdec, and if lbjpeg does not exist then its code belongs back in lbmthp.
decomp_lessons: Do not infer a separate library/object solely from JPEG-related functionality; in Melee this JPEG decode code can be part of the THP video decoder object.
When correcting a split, update all three layers together: assembly file ownership, symbol namespace, and obj_files.mk entries.
If an apparently standalone lb module only wraps or drives THP/JPEG video decode behavior, verify whether it is actually a tail region of lbmthp before creating a separate lbjpeg object.
File-local evidence and neighboring symbols can outweigh semantic guesses from generic technology names like JPEG.
Large assembly moves can still be low-risk when the code bytes/addresses are preserved and the main change is object ownership plus symbol namespace.
naming_conventions: Use THPDec_* for address-named routines and data that live in asm/dolphin/thp/THPDec.s, replacing misleading JPEG_* or JPEGDEC_* prefixes.
Use THPInit for the THP decoder initialization routine at 80335C58 rather than JPEG_80335C58.
Do not keep an lbJpeg_* namespace when the evidence says the module does not exist; merged labels became lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0 in this PR.
Callsites should reflect the owning module namespace after a split correction, even when the branch targets and addresses are unchanged.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved a large contiguous assembly block from asm/dolphin/jpeg/jpegdec.s into asm/dolphin/thp/THPDec.s without changing instruction addresses, preserving match stability while correcting file boundaries.
Renamed internal branch/call targets consistently after the move so calls inside THPDec.s target THPDec_* labels rather than deleted JPEG_* labels.
Merged the small lbjpeg assembly into lbmthp.s at the matching address range, then redirected external gm_1A36.s references to the new lbMthp labels.
Removed obsolete object entries from obj_files.mk after moving assembly so link order/object membership matches the corrected split.
Kept raw address-derived names for unknown THPDec routines where better semantic names were not evidenced, except for the clearly identified THPInit.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only explicit rationale came from the PR body: evidence in the file indicated the code should be thpdec, and lbjpeg therefore does not exist and was merged back into lbmthp.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that decompile or rename THPDec_8032F8D4, THPDec_8032FD40, THPDec_8032FDB8, THPDec_80331340, or THPDec_803313D0.
Search for references to lbMthp8001F890, lbMthp8001F928, and lbMthp8001FAA0 to see whether they later received semantic names.
Check whether any remaining JPEG_* or JPEGDEC_* labels in the project are legitimate standalone JPEG code or should also be THPDec-owned.
Review later obj_files.mk split changes around dolphin/thp and melee/lb to ensure this corrected module boundary was preserved.
